title: Hay-Carren
content:
'Tis merry ov a zummer's day,
When vo'k be out a-haulï¿½n hay,
Where boughs, a-spread upon the ground,
Do meï¿½ke the staddle big an' round;
An' grass do stand in pook, or lie
In long-backed weï¿½les or parsels, dry.
There I do vind it stir my heart
To hear the frothï¿½n hosses snort,
A-haulï¿½n on, wi' sleek heï¿½ir'd hides,
The red-wheel'd waggon's deep-blue zides.
Aye; let me have woone cup o' drink,
An' hear the linky harness clink,
An' then my blood do run so warm,
An' put sich strangth 'ithin my eï¿½rm,
That I do long to toss a pick,
A-pitchï¿½n or a-meï¿½kï¿½n rick.

The bwoy is at the hosse's head,
An' up upon the waggon bed
The lwoaders, strong o' eï¿½rm do stan',
At head, an' back at taï¿½l, a man,
Wi' skill to build the lwoad upright
An' bind the vwolded corners tight;
An' at each zide o'm, sprack an' strong,
A pitcher wi' his long-stem'd prong,
Avore the best two women now
A-call'd to reï¿½ky after plough.

When I do pitchy, 'tis my pride
Vor Jenny Hine to reï¿½ke my zide,
An' zee her fling her reï¿½ke, an' reach
So vur, an' teï¿½ke in sich a streech;
An' I don't shatter hay, an' meï¿½ke
Mwore work than needs vor Jenny's reï¿½ke.
I'd sooner zee the weï¿½les' high rows
Lik' hedges up above my nose,
Than have light work myzelf, an' vind
Poor Jeï¿½ne a-beï¿½t an' left behind;
Vor she would sooner drop down dead,
Than let the pitchers get a-head.

'Tis merry at the rick to zee
How picks do wag, an' hay do vlee.
While woone's unlwoadï¿½n, woone do teï¿½ke
The pitches in; an' zome do meï¿½ke
The lofty rick upright an' roun',
An' tread en hard, an' reï¿½ke en down,
An' tip en, when the zun do zet,
To shoot a sudden vall o' wet.
An' zoo 'tis merry any day
Where vo'k be out a-carrï¿½n hay.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Barnes represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of William Barnes's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
